entries.definition:
كتاب الفاء

فزع

(فسد) الفاء والسين والدال كلمةٌ واحدة، فَسَدَ الشَّيءُ يَفْسُد فساداً وفُسوداً، وهو فاسِدٌ وفَسِيد.

فسر - فشج - فشخ - فشل - فشا

room, and God will make room for you.
تَفَسَّح tafassaḥ [imper. of v. V, intrans.] make room for one
another (58:11) يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا فِي الْمَجَالِسِ فَافْسَحُوا
you who believe, if it is said to you, ‘Make room for one another!’ in
assemblies, then make room.
entries.definition_english:
room, and God will make room for you.
تَفَسَّح tafassaḥ [imper. of v. V, intrans.] make room for one
another (58:11) يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قِيلَ لَكُمْ تَفَسَّحُوا فِي الْمَجَالِسِ فَافْسَحُوا
you who believe, if it is said to you, ‘Make room for one another!’ in
assemblies, then make room.

entries.definition:
f–s–d to corrupt, to spoil, to decay, to fall into disorder, to be
perverted, to be wicked; to make trouble. Of this root, five forms
occur 50 times in the Qur’an: فَسَدَ fasada three times; أَفْسَدَ ʾafsada
15 times; فَسَاد fasād 11 times; مُفْسِد mufsid once and مُفْسِدُون
mufsidūn 20 times.

فَسَدَ fasada i/u [v. intrans.] to become corrupt, to fall into
disorder (2:251) وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ اللَّهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ لَفَسَدَتِ الْأَرْضُ if it were
not for God’s repelling some by means of others the Earth would
become corrupted.

أَفْسَدَ ʾafsada [v. IV] I [with prep. فِي, intrans.] to act corruptly,
to play havoc, to cause damage (2:30) وَإِذْ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ إِنِّي جَاعِلٌ فِي
الْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً قَالُوا أَتَجْعَلُ فِيهَا مَنْ يُفْسِدُ فِيهَا when your Lord told the angels,
‘I am putting a successor/representative on earth’, they said,
‘How can You place on it someone who will cause damage?’ II
[trans.] to corrupt, to throw into disorder, to ruin (27:34) إِنَّ الْمُلُوكَ
إِذَا دَخَلُوا قَرْيَةً أَفْسَدُوهَا وَجَعَلُوا أَعِزَّةَ أَهْلِهَا أَذِلَّةً whenever kings/wealthy
people enter a city, they corrupt it and make the noblest of its
inhabitants debased.’

فَسَاد fasād [v. n./n.] 1 mischief, wrongness, destruction,
causing corruption (5:32) مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسًا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ فَسَادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ
النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا whoever kills a person–not [in retribution] for [the
killing of] another nor for spreading corruption in the land–it is
as if he has killed all humankind 2 disorder, corruption (8:73) وَالَّذِينَ
كَفَرُوا بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ إِلَّا تَفْعَلُوهُ تَكُنْ فِتْنَةٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَفَسَادٌ كَبِيرٌ those who
disbelieve are allies of one another–if you do not do it [what you
have been commanded to do], there will be persecution in the
land and great corruption 3 physical damage, drought, death,
malfunction, scarcity of goods (30:41) ظَهَرَ الْفَسَادُ فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ بِمَا
كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِي النَّاسِ corruption has appeared on land and sea

[everywhere] because of what people’s own hands have earned
[i.e. done].

مُفْسِد mufsid [act. part.; pl. مُفْسِدُون mufsidūn] mischievous,
destructive, corrupting person, someone who causes disruption,
someone who spreads corruption, ruining, causing corruption
(18:94) إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ indeed Gog and Magog are
causing destruction in the land.

destructive, corrupting person, someone who causes disruption,

(18:94) إِنَّ يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ مُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ indeed Gog and Magog are
